timeline_html:
Summary. Beata’s shedding began over a year ago and has continued to worsen. Several stress-related events and postpartum hormonal shifts seem to have overlapped, keeping the scalp in a prolonged reactive phase.

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Pregnancy, birth, or miscarriage (resolved; Nov 2024–Sep 2025)
  • Major emotional stress (ongoing; started Sep 2025)
  • Chronic stress (ongoing; started Nov 2025)

Timeline forecast. The postpartum trigger would have peaked around Dec 2025 – Jan 2026, with shedding normally easing by Apr–Jul 2026. Early regrowth would typically appear from May–Jul 2026, though continuing emotional and chronic stress may delay this. Dominant driver: Ongoing chronic stress (since Nov 2025) because it persists beyond the resolved postpartum phase and aligns with the period when shedding stayed high.

Where you are now. You’re still in the stabilization phase—the worst shedding has likely passed, but recovery is slowed by sustained stress exposure. This phase can feel discouraging, yet once stress levels lighten, regrowth tends to follow within a few months. 🌱
